channel.title: Dataverse on Ibrahim Stephenson
channel.description: Recent content in Dataverse on Ibrahim Stephenson

item.title: When Power Automate Isn't Enough, There Are Plugins
item.description:
Like most people, the first thing I did as a Power Platform developer was build a flow. Watched it run. That green tick, the satisfaction of knowing you’ve just saved someone time somewhere.

Since then I’ve built somewhere around 200 of them.

item.title: How I Built a Proper ALM Pipeline for Power Platform (Starting From Chaos)
item.description:
I used to dread our sprint deployments.

Every two weeks we deployed our solutions. Individually. Unmanaged. One person’s change overwrote someone else’s. Hotfixes, redeployments, environment restorations. It was chaos. Unmanaged chaos.

This is how I fixed it, and what it cost me.

item.title: The Datascraper
item.description:
At my company, the Datascraper was a thing of legend. Believed to have been lost. Forgotten.

Until I got my hands on it.

item.title: Generative Pages in Model-Driven Apps: A Practical Guide
item.description: If you haven’t been living under a rock you should have heard about generative pages by now. Essentially it’s a full React page created using prompts, no code needed, no drag and drop. Nothing. Just talk to the AI and it will create a functional site.

item.title: Building a Self-Service Knowledge Portal That Runs Itself
item.description: The worst kind of support ticket is the one that didn’t need to be raised.

item.title: How I Fixed a Two-Year Data Bug That Was Costing Thousands Every Month
item.description: We had an issue that was affecting us for 2 years.
